author: codex-gpt5
source_text: Service A holds lock X then waits on Y; Service B holds Y then waits on X. The deadlock manifests after a retry-storm under load. Root-causing requires tracing both lock-acquisition orders and the retry backoff schedule.
clone_text:
^grammar:v0.3
# expected-to-LOSE: refuse dense, exit to plain
@svcA := service:A
@svcB := service:B
^plain<<<
Service A acquires lock X and then blocks waiting for lock Y.
Service B already holds Y and is waiting for X, so neither side can make progress.
This only surfaces under a retry storm; root-causing needs a full lock-order trace, not a compressed encoding.
>>>^plain
